author: bridge-bot
content:
<sebastiengllmt> is there any document that talks about what Ethereum addresses will look like in a post-quantum world?
i.e. will it still be a keccak hash of a public key (just of some post-quantum scheme's public key), or will the address format visually change?

author: _shemnon
content:
Nothing is fixed, but all the proposals I've seen are still trimmed keccak hashes of the public key, differing only on wether domain separation bytes are applied.

Changing the address touches on an old but still live issue about "Address Space Expansion" - the key barrier being any EVM opcode taking an address trims the register to the bottom twenty bytes. Some contracts have taken advantage of that to pack data and not clean it up before use, so simply expanding those opcodes it is not a zero-impact change.

My thought is the correct place to address this is when zkVM guest programs are adopted, as it's a fresh system with no baggage. There are a number of ways to map this into EVM when calling an EVM contract that are best examined at the correct time.
